overview: The Village of Weston Community Life and Public Safety Committee met on May 4th, addressing several ordinance amendments and policy matters. Key actions included recommending approval of nuisance code amendments, an e-bike ordinance with emoto restrictions added, mobile food vendor fire inspection requirements, address corrections, and a raze order for an abandoned property at 4104 Shore Avenue.
committee: Community Life and Public Safety Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Approval of minutes from previous meeting
Mobile food vendors fire inspection requirements

discussions.body: The committee finalized proposed amendments to Chapter 50 regarding nuisances, including tree limb height requirements over streets and walkways (14 and 10 feet specifications were switched as previously discussed). Committee member Olsen made a motion to recommend approval to the board of trustees, seconded by Campkey. Motion passed unanimously.

The committee discussed the proposed e-bike ordinance for Chapter 82 traffic and vehicles. Chief Hunt advocated strongly for prohibiting emotos (electric dirt bike-style vehicles) from roadways, sidewalks, and trails, comparing them to unlicensed dirt bikes. The committee discussed scooters and agreed to leave enforcement discretion to officers rather than adding specific exemptions. Olsen made a motion to recommend approval with the addition that emotos are prohibited from roadways, sidewalks, trails, and any public property in the village. Seconded by Sherwanka, motion passed unanimously.

Trustee Tolson presented research showing Weston's 12-hour parking restriction window (4 AM to 4 PM, October 31 to May 1) is significantly longer than surrounding municipalities, which typically have 5-hour windows. Chief Hunt expressed preference for keeping overnight parking restrictions to help clear roads before plowing begins. Michael from DPW suggested eliminating the even/odd requirement and instead signing specific problem areas (approximately 6-8 locations with multifamily housing). The committee decided to defer to July, asking Michael to identify specific exception areas.

Mount Olive Church requested a crosswalk on Alderson Street between their church and DC Everest school parking lot. Michael estimated a basic crosswalk would cost approximately $2,000-$3,000, while a lighted system would cost $25,000-$30,000. The committee was reluctant to set precedent for paying for requested crosswalks. Olsen made a motion that if Mount Olive is willing to cover the cost of installing the crosswalk, the committee recommends board approval. Seconded by John Sherwanka, motion passed unanimously.

Chief Hunt presented a regional fire inspection program for mobile food vendors involving Wisconsin Rapids, Marshfield, Stevens Point, Plover, Rib Mountain, and Safer. Under this program, any participating department can inspect a food truck and apply a sticker valid for all participating municipalities, eliminating redundant inspections. Fire Marshal Marty already conducted one inspection that failed due to improper LP piping and unsecured propane containers. Sherwanka made a motion to approve Ordinance 26-8, seconded by Campkey. Motion passed unanimously.

Planning staff presented three address corrections needed for E911 compliance: 5915 Delicowski Street (duplex needing second address 5917), Ross Avenue properties requiring resequencing due to an existing apartment building already addressed as 3909, and 2211 Radkey Avenue needing an even number for a new duplex on a private drive. Chief Hunt also identified Weston Villa Apartments on Delicowski as having improper addressing with eight separate addresses for one building. Motion to recommend approval passed unanimously.

Staff requested authorization to begin a raze order for an abandoned structure at 4104 Shore Avenue. The property has been abandoned since 2010-2011 and was previously considered for a fire training burn but was deemed too unsafe. The structure was found to be housing chickens when investigated several years ago. Current owners prefer the village proceed with demolition with costs added to the tax roll. Olsen made the motion, seconded by Campkey. Motion passed unanimously.

publicComment: Jim Senalt spoke during public comment, expressing concerns about the new farmers market location at a park. He stated it was 'a terrible execution' and predicted the grass would be destroyed by summer's end. He questioned whether parking vehicles on grass violates village code and raised concerns about ADA-compliant handicap parking being placed on grass rather than asphalt. Senalt also questioned the need for new fire inspections for food trucks and asked why mobile food vendors couldn't set up independently at the park on non-farmers market days. He criticized the age of some code violation cases, noting some have been on the list for years without visible progress.
actionItems: Chapter 50 nuisance amendments recommended for board approval with tree limb height corrections
E-bike ordinance recommended for board approval with emoto prohibition on roadways, sidewalks, trails, and public property added
Overnight parking regulations deferred to July meeting; Michael to identify specific problem areas needing signage
Crosswalk for Alderson Street conditionally recommended if Mount Olive Church pays installation costs; Michael to contact them
Ordinance 26-8 for mobile food vendor fire inspections recommended for board approval
Address corrections for Delicowski Street, Ross Avenue, and Radkey Avenue recommended for board approval
Raze order for 4104 Shore Avenue authorized to proceed
Staff to investigate property at Sternberg and South Timber for potential code violations
title: Community Life and Public Safety
